howItWorks.intro: This sphere, cone & cylinder calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How do you calculate the volume of a sphere?
faq.answer: The volume of a sphere is V = (4/3)πr³. Cube the radius, multiply by π, then multiply by 4/3. For example, a sphere with radius 5 has volume (4/3)π(125) ≈ 523.6 cubic units.

faq.question: What is the slant height of a cone?
faq.answer: The slant height is the distance from the tip of the cone to any point on the edge of the base circle. It can be found using the Pythagorean theorem: slant = √(r² + h²), where r is the radius and h is the height.

faq.question: How are cone and cylinder volumes related?
faq.answer: A cone's volume is exactly one-third of a cylinder with the same radius and height. V(cone) = ⅓ × V(cylinder) = ⅓πr²h.

faq.question: Does the sphere formula use height?
faq.answer: No. A sphere is defined entirely by its radius. The volume formula V = (4/3)πr³ and surface area formula SA = 4πr² both depend only on the radius.
